Displays with varying color shades some darker than others were detected on the same pallet. The darker ones are being separated out; the lighter displays will be processed first to avoid mixing them, and once the lighter-shaded displays are exhausted, the darker ones will be used.

Grey Boscan 
How is everything going?

Tempering chart test check update 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Flow wrap packaging test check update 


I checked the flow-wrapped out of the 24 pieces in the display, none were found to be open or to have the small holes sometimes seen at the product corners. The product is properly sealed, looks good, and features a clear, legible "best if used by" print

Thanks David for the update.

After counting the beads for one chain we the number is: 
180 beads.

And there are 10 total chains so 1800 beads. 
This would be a difficult task to perform before and after every run. 

Also, there are a few areas that could be a concern in terms of the chains staying attached:

1. They have to be thread on tightly. 
2. Each one has a washer, those can be easily misplaced or overlooked. 
3. At the ends they can slide off. So when transporting between cleans it’ll have to be moved and even stored horizontally or have the chains removed and stored separately. 

Just a few thoughts as Joe and I looked it over.
Great detail Conner Boyd

I counted all the beads in the 10 rows. 8 of the strands had 180 beads and 2 of the strands had 183 beads. I asked maintenence to cut off 3 beads on those 2 so all the strands have 180 beads. It took approximately 30 min to count them FYI
Kathleen Gilbert Curt Willis Charity Young Jorel Gourdin Chad Stone Joseph Scott
